stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Modern art historians studying Michelangelo's techniques have discovered that his preliminary sketches reveal an artist who was constantly revising and improving his initial concepts.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Modern art historians studying Michelangelo's techniques have discovered that his preliminary sketches reveal an artist whom was constantly revising and improving his initial concepts.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Modern art historians studying Michelangelo's techniques have discovered that his preliminary sketches reveal an artist which was constantly revising and improving his initial concepts.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Modern art historians studying Michelangelo's techniques have discovered that his preliminary sketches reveal an artist that was constantly revising and improving his initial concepts.

explanation: Choice A is correct because 'who' is the appropriate relative pronoun to use when referring to a person (the artist) in the subject position of the relative clause. The relative pronoun 'who' serves as the subject of the verb 'was' in the clause 'who was constantly revising and improving his initial concepts.' This construction follows standard English conventions for relative pronoun usage with human antecedents in subject position.

distractorRationale.B: 'Whom' is incorrect here because it is the object form of the relative pronoun, but the relative pronoun serves as the subject of the clause 'was constantly revising.' 'Whom' should only be used when the relative pronoun functions as an object.
distractorRationale.C: 'Which' is incorrect because it refers to things or animals, not people. When the antecedent is a person, standard English requires 'who' or 'whom,' not 'which.'
distractorRationale.D: While 'that' can sometimes refer to people in restrictive relative clauses, it is less preferred in formal writing when 'who' is available, and in this context, 'who' is the more appropriate and conventional choice for referring to the artist as a person.

passage: The Renaissance artist Michelangelo, whose sculptures and paintings have influenced countless generations of artists, was known not only for his technical mastery but also for his perfectionist tendencies. His contemporaries often remarked that he would spend months refining a single detail, believing that each element of a work must contribute to its overall harmony. This meticulous approach, while time-consuming, resulted in masterpieces that continue to inspire awe centuries later. Modern art historians studying Michelangelo's techniques have discovered that his preliminary sketches reveal an artist who was constantly revising and improving his initial concepts.
